channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PPC raises concerns over potential import dumping after AfriSam acquisition by Chinese firm
item.description: South Africa’s cement sector is facing fresh concerns following the Competition Commission’s approval of West China Cement (WCC) acquiring local competitor AfriSam for R2.5 billion. PPC, one of the co...

item.title: House Prosecution Panel Assures Senate Tensions Won’t Delay VP Sara Duterte Impeachment Process
item.description: A member of the House prosecution team handling the impeachment case of Vice President Sara Duterte expressed confidence that recent tensions in the Senate will not impact the impeachment process. Man...

item.title: Kathy Kiuna Celebrates Son Jeremy's Master’s Graduation in Finance
item.description: Lady Bishop Kathy Kiuna is over the moon after her son, Jeremy Kiuna, successfully graduated with a Master's degree in Finance. Jeremy has often credited his late father, Bishop Allan Kiuna, for inspi...

item.title: Kericho Youth Secures Overseas Job in Dubai with Support from Well-Wisher
item.description: Willy, a young certified electrician from Chemosot in Kericho County, has embarked on a new chapter of his life after securing employment in Dubai, thanks to the intervention of a local well-wisher. H...

item.title: Latrell Mitchell ruled out of State of Origin I due to back injury; NSW finalises squad
item.description: NSW have suffered a major setback ahead of State of Origin I as Latrell Mitchell has withdrawn himself from selection due to a back injury. Mitchell, who was injured in Friday night’s loss against the...

item.title: Wike’s Loyalists Dominate APC Primaries for House of Representatives in Rivers State
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) in Rivers State has concluded its primary elections for the House of Representatives seats across the 13 federal constituencies. Key allies of FCT Minister Nyesom W...

item.title: Analysis of U.S.-Iran Conflict Reveals Strategic and Economic Strains
item.description: The U.S. war on Iran, often portrayed by officials as a swift success, appears far costlier and less effective than public rhetoric suggests. Reporting from major outlets including CNN, The Washington...

item.title: Dorcas Rigathi Explains the Symbolism Behind DCP Party Logo and Party Values
item.description: Pastor Dorcas Rigathi has explained the meaning behind the Democracy for Citizens Party (DCP) logo months after the party's launch in Kenya. Critics had argued that the party logo lacked significance ...

item.title: French designer Lucas Lecacheur reinvents surfboards with functional, experimental designs
item.description: French designer Lucas Lecacheur is shaking up the world of surfing with his bold and unconventional surfboard designs. Raised on the French island Île de Ré, Lecacheur has surfed since childhood and p...

item.title: Parliament braces for potential delays in Ramaphosa’s Phala Phala impeachment process
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is expected to encounter significant opposition in parliament should he attempt to interdict the ongoing Phala Phala impeachment process. Opposition parties outside of the Go...

item.title: Balancing Safety and Efficiency in South Africa's Construction Industry
item.description: Falls from height continue to be the leading cause of fatalities in the South African construction sector. Despite strict regulations, unsafe practices remain common on many sites. Recent incidents in...

item.title: Thom Hartmann Urges Democrats to Prioritize Anti-Corruption Message Against Trump While Cleaning Up Their Own House
item.description: In this opinion piece, progressive commentator Thom Hartmann argues that widespread corruption within the Trump administration presents a major political opportunity for Democrats ahead of the 2026 mi...

item.title: Regina festival features demonstrations of Scottish martial arts
item.description: The annual Highland Gathering and Celtic Festival in Regina recently introduced festival-goers to Scottish martial arts, offering demonstrations and workshops to engage both enthusiasts and newcomers....

item.title: Mike Sonko and Raila Odinga Recognised at Heroes of Africa Awards for Service to Society
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko and the late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga were recognised at the Heroes of Africa Awards, which were held at the Kenyatta International Conference Centre (KICC...

item.title: SERAP Urges INEC to Investigate Alleged N800bn Misuse by APC Governors for Campaigns
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) don call on the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) to sharply investigate claims say some governors for the All Progressives ...

item.title: An Insight into the Princess of Wales’s Personal Expenses and Lifestyle
item.description: The Princess of Wales, Kate Middleton, maintains a carefully managed personal budget funded largely by profits from the Duchy of Cornwall, a private estate generating around £22 million annually. Whil...

item.title: Fremantle Dockers Secure Ninth Consecutive Win with Strong Performance Against Essendon at MCG
item.description: Fremantle, also known as Walyalup for Sir Doug Nicholls Round, secured a record-equalling ninth consecutive win with a dominant 104-61 victory over Essendon at the MCG. Pat Voss starred with five goal...

item.title: Celebrating the Life and Legacy of Silvia Pinal in Mexican Cinema
item.description: Silvia Pinal is one of the most influential figures in Latin American entertainment, recognized as the “Last Diva” of Mexico’s Golden Age of Cinema. Her career spans multiple decades and showcases rem...

item.title: Ramaphosa to Seek Court Interdict to Delay Phala Phala Impeachment Proceedings
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is preparing to challenge the impeachment process stemming from the Phala Phala scandal through legal channels. According to sources close to the president, he intends to see...

item.title: UK Announces Updates to the 1990 Computer Misuse Act to Protect Security Researchers and Strengthen Cybercrime Laws
item.description: The United Kingdom is moving forward with long-awaited reforms to the Computer Misuse Act of 1990, a law originally passed in response to a high-profile hacking incident involving the Duke of Edinburg...
